title: Neonatal Cardiorespiratory Monitoring
authors: Di Fiore JM, Raffay TM / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Cardiorespiratory Monitoring?
Monitor ที่เราเห็นทุกวันที่หัวเตียง NICU ไม่ได้ 'อ่านได้เลย' — มี pitfall เต็มไปหมด: (1) Pulse oximetry averaging time 16 sec = underestimates event nadir และ merges short events → false sense of security; (2) SpO2 <88%: accuracy drops — mean bias -1.84±2.93%, <40% of readings within 3% of SaO2 (1664 preterm infants); (3) Impedance apnea monitor MISSES obstructive apnea 100% — no alarm ขณะที่ทารกอาจ desaturate อยู่; (4) NIRS ≠ SpO2: measures tissue oxygenation (venous-weighted), NOT arterial — normal rSO2 ≠ normal SaO2; (5) HeRO system: HRV regression model ลด VLBW mortality โดย early warning sepsis ก่อน clinical deterioration; (6) home pulse oximeter sensitivity: 0–88.8% for hypoxemia (ต้อง counsel parents). เข้าใจ physics + limitation → ใช้ monitor ได้ถูก decision.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบายหลักการทำงานของ ECG monitoring ใน NICU: electrode types, AHA electrical safety limits, HeRO system สำหรับ early sepsis detection
เปรียบเทียบ cardiac output monitoring ใน neonates: Fick principle (gold standard), TTE, Doppler, electrical velocimetry, SVC/descending aortic flow
ระบุข้อจำกัดของ noninvasive blood pressure monitoring ใน NICU: oscillometry accuracy ที่ MAP ≤30 mmHg, cuff sizing rules
อธิบายหลักการ pulse oximetry: Beer-Lambert law, wavelengths, plethysmogram, ผลของ averaging time ต่อ event detection
ระบุ optimal alarm settings สำหรับ pulse oximetry ใน preterm: SpO2 target, alarm delay, averaging time — อิงหลักฐาน
เปรียบเทียบ PtcO2 vs pulse oximetry: response time, accuracy range, skin risk, rotation protocol, ข้อบ่งใช้
อธิบาย NIRS cerebral oximetry: rSO2 formula, normal values (term vs preterm), ข้อจำกัด, ผล BORN trial
เปรียบเทียบ end-tidal CO2 vs transcutaneous CO2: sidestream vs mainstream, accuracy ใน pulmonary disease, HFOV, colorimetric ETT confirmation
อธิบาย respiratory monitoring modalities: impedance (limitation ต่อ obstructive apnea), RIP (detection obstructive), EIT, dEMG
สรุป future directions: smart alarms, waveform storage, automated feedback controllers, wireless sensor technology
1. Hemodynamic Monitoring
1.1 Electrocardiogram (ECG)
Electrode types: silver-silver chloride, foil-based, recessed, floating — ทุกประเภทมี electrolyte gel
ELBW skin protection: minimize multiple probe applications → ↑ adhesiveness, ↓ skin damage; ↑ transepidermal water loss (TEWL) จาก fragile skin preterm
Next-gen: ultrathin epidermal electronic systems — wireless ECG, ติดด้วยน้ำ — ยังอยู่ใน investigation

AHA Electrical Safety Limits — ECG Monitoring
Two criteria: (1) Current through patient-connected leads: ≤10 μA (microamperes) — prevents ventricular fibrillation. (2) Chassis leakage current: <100 μA; optimal ≤10 μA. Current applied to chest wall เพื่อ detect small electrical changes ต่อ heartbeat — monitor ต้องออกแบบมาให้ไม่เกิน limits นี้. ควรทราบว่า infant มี limited surface area → electrode placement critical.
Heart Rate Variability (HRV) — HeRO System
HeRO System: longitudinal regression-based model ของ abnormal HRV patterns ที่ associate กับ systemic infection + inflammation
Clinical outcome: ↓ mortality ใน VLBW infants — early warning of patient distress days before clinical deterioration
ยังอยู่ใน research: cross-correlation of HR กับ vital signs อื่น; sophisticated mathematical analyses → detect late-onset sepsis, NEC
1.2 Cardiac Output Monitoring

Fick Principle — Gold Standard (Research Setting)
CO = VO₂ / (Ca − Cv) — oxygen consumption / (arterial O₂ content − mixed venous O₂ content). Accurate especially in low-flow states แต่ limited by air leakage, cardiopulmonary disease, ↑ pulmonary O₂ consumption. Invasive + requires special setup → impractical at bedside. Noninvasive validated alternatives ใน neonates: transcutaneous Doppler, TTE, thoracic electrical impedance.
Transthoracic Echocardiography (TTE)
LV output (TTE): comparable to PA thermodilution and O₂-Fick methods — best-validated noninvasive method
SVC flow: blood from brain + upper body → surrogate for cerebral blood flow. High error percentage; wide variation in reference ranges → limited clinical application
Descending aortic flow (below ductus): systemic flow to lower body. Absence of end-diastolic flow or reversal → hemodynamically significant L→R PDA shunt
Transcutaneous Doppler
Ultrasound beam measures blood flow velocity → velocity-time waveform → stroke distance = area under curve
CO = stroke distance × cross-sectional area of vessel × heart rate
Wide variation in results → limit to trend monitoring only — not absolute CO measurement
Electrical Velocimetry
SV = Vept × LVET⁻¹ × LVET: Vept = volume of electrically participating tissue (from height/weight); LVET⁻¹ = ohmic equivalent of mean aortic blood velocity; LVET = LV ejection time
Electrode placement: forehead, left neck, left mid-axillary at xiphoid, left thigh
Comparable to echo for LV output measurement, แต่ individual variation exists with both techniques
1.3 Blood Pressure Monitoring
Invasive (Gold Standard)
Indwelling arterial catheter (UAC/UVC): continuous direct readings — gold standard
Transducer เปลี่ยน fluid pressure → voltage. ต้องทำ: (1) flush dome (no air bubbles); (2) place transducer at level of catheter tip; (3) zero pressure before use
Transducer higher than heart → erroneously low BP readings
Noninvasive — Oscillometry (Most Common NICU)

Oscillometric Blood Pressure — Principle และ Limitation ใน Preterm
Principle: cuff inflation → occlusion → slow deflation → small pulsations detected as cuff approaches systolic pressure → oscillations increase → peak = mean arterial pressure → decline. Accuracy: GOOD in critically ill preterm with normal BP, แต่ accuracy ลดลงมากเมื่อ MAP ≤30 mmHg. โดยทั่วไป noninvasive tends to OVERESTIMATE vs arterial line, especially in smaller infants at lower BP. Cuff width: ~40% of limb circumference (AHA). Too narrow or loose → falsely HIGH readings. Automated transient readings; new wireless devices using binodal epidermal pulse arrival time under investigation.
Auscultatory method: NOT practical in neonates — Korotkoff sounds inaudible, intra-observer variability, disturbs patient
2. Blood Gas Monitoring — Oxygenation
2.1 Pulse Oximetry

Pulse Oximetry — Beer-Lambert Principle
Beer-Lambert law: concentration of absorbing substance ∝ intensity of transmitted light. Pulse oximetry ใช้ differential light absorption ของ deoxygenated Hb (Hb): absorbs red light, 660 nm และ oxygenated HbO₂: absorbs infrared light, 940 nm. Probe: 2 LEDs (660 + 940 nm) + photodiode detector opposite side. SpO₂ = ratio of pulsatile/constant component at 660 nm vs 940 nm. Response time 3–4 seconds (lung → peripheral sensor). No heating, no calibration. Perfusion index available → alerts to low perfusion (hypothermia, ↓CO, vasoconstriction).
AAP Targets & Alarm Settings

rows: AAP recommended; balance hypoxia vs hyperoxia
Avoid O₂ excess; compliance with AAP target
Trigger FiO₂ ↑ in small increments; ↓ hypoxemia
Reduce short-event alarms, preserve real events

blocks.text: Averaging Time — ความสำคัญทางคลินิก
Averaging time <8 sec: nuisance alarms ↑ จาก motion + short self-resolving events
Averaging time >10 sec: distorts SpO₂ waveform: eliminates true short events, reduces nadir severity, merges closely-occurring events → longer events → more likely treated with O₂ (undesired)
8 sec: optimal balance — reduces alarms from short (<20 sec) self-resolving hypoxemia while minimizing falsely prolonged events
15-sec alarm delay: ↓ number of alarms by 67% (Di Fiore data) — allows spontaneous recovery vs continuous FiO₂ increases
Accuracy Limitations
SpO₂ <88%: mean difference SaO₂ − SpO₂ = −1.84 ± 2.93%; <40% of infants within 3% of SaO₂ when SpO₂ <88% (study of 1664 preterm infants)
Ambient light: SpO₂ trends toward 85% (ratio R/IR = 1) under excess ambient light; cover extremity + direct opposition emitter/detector
Fetal hemoglobin (HbF): left shift O₂-dissociation curve → normal SpO₂ may NOT reflect adequate tissue O₂ delivery. Blood transfusion ↓ HbF proportion — reassess O₂ threshold post-transfusion
Skin melanin: in neonates: minimal effect (unlike adults); possible modest bias toward SpO₂ overestimation in Black infants (small magnitude)
Functional vs fractional SpO₂: differ by ~2% (equivalent to COHb + MetHb in healthy non-smoking adults) — manufacturer difference
Home baby monitors: sensitivity 0–88.8% for hypoxemia; 0% for bradycardia vs hospital Masimo Radical 7 — NOT FDA regulated; counsel parents accordingly
2.2 Transcutaneous Oxygen (PtcO₂)
Principle: platinum cathode + silver reference anode in electrolyte + O₂-permeable membrane → O₂ from heated skin reacts with Pt/AgCl → electrical current → PO₂
Target temperature: 44°C; lower → underestimates PaO₂
Rotation: every 2–4 hours — even 2 hrs associated with hyperpigmented macules + prolonged erythema in preterm with fragile skin
Response delay: 16–30 seconds (O₂ diffusion through capillaries + membrane) vs 3–4 sec for pulse oximetry
Heating prep time: ~15 minutes before reliable values — large % of monitoring time without O₂ data if site relocated frequently

PtcO₂ Accuracy Ranges — ใช้ร่วมกับ ABG เสมอ
Accuracy: ~15% of hypoxemic + hyperoxemic events MISSED by PtcO₂. Sensitivity for hypoxemia (<50 mmHg): 68–100%; for hyperoxemia (>80 mmHg): 71–92% — wide variability. PtcO₂ ดีกว่า SpO₂: continuous + stable during motion. PtcO₂ ด้อยกว่า SpO₂: slow response, long prep time, skin burn risk, reduced reliable data period. Clinical rule: ALWAYS confirm PtcO₂ with ABG periodically, especially during supplemental O₂ changes.
2.3 Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (NIRS)

NIRS — Regional Tissue Oxygenation ≠ Arterial Saturation
NIRS measures tissue (venous-weighted) oxygenation: rSO₂ = HbO₂ / (HbO₂ + HHb) — oxygen delivered MINUS oxygen extracted by tissue. Chromophores: cytochrome aa₃, myoglobin, HbO₂ + HHb. Wavelengths 700–1300 nm. Probe: one temple → photons detected at opposite temple. Reference signal corrects for skin reflection, laser drift, skin color. Spatial resolution ~1 cm²; limited to cortical surface.
Normal Values และ Clinical Evidence
Normal term newborn: rSO₂ = 77.9% ± 8.5%
Extremely preterm: normative range 55–85%; influenced by GA, body position, postnatal age
ISO standard: recently issued by International Organization for Standardization → ↑ comparability between manufacturers (prior: poor reproducibility)

BORN trial (key RCT): treatment guided by cerebral oximetry for first 72 h: NOT associated with ↓ death or severe brain injury at 36 wk PMA vs usual care — clinical role ยังไม่ชัดเจน; more research needed
Association of rSO₂ with IVH, cognitive impairment, mortality: mixed results across studies
Limitations: poor inter-manufacturer reproducibility (before ISO); absolute rSO₂ interpretation still under research
3. Carbon Dioxide Monitoring
3.1 End-Tidal CO₂ Monitoring (Petco₂)
Principle: IR spectroscopy — CO₂ absorbs light at 4.26 μm wavelength; amount absorbed ∝ CO₂ concentration
Sidestream vs Mainstream

rows: In-line cuvette; IR system over chamber
Underestimates in low Vt; microstream: 15 μL sample, 50 mL/min

blocks.text: Clinical Accuracy Considerations
Healthy newborns: Petco₂ accurately estimates alveolar CO₂
Pulmonary disease (BPD, atelectasis, V/Q mismatch): agreement POOR + negatively influenced by disease severity — underestimates PaCO₂
HFOV: standard capnography prohibited — high frequency, small Vt; microstream through double-lumen ETT with HFOV software = promising but limited evidence
Neonates have ↑ RR + ↓ Vt → wide CO₂ variation; underestimation common
Colorimetric CO₂ Detector — ETT Confirmation

Colorimetric Detector — ETT Placement Verification
pH-sensitive indicator: metacresol purple. Purple → Yellow when CO₂ ≥0.54% (0.5 kPa) to 2–5%. Placed between ventilator and ETT. Role: efficient confirmation of correct ETT placement in newborn. CANNOT detect: (1) hypocarbia, (2) hypercarbia, (3) right mainstem bronchus intubation, (4) oropharyngeal placement in spontaneous breathing patients. Clinical pearl: neonatal extubation failure devastating → colorimetric detector reduces risk but has clear limitations.
3.2 Transcutaneous CO₂ (PtcCO₂)
Principle: pH-sensitive glass electrode + silver chloride reference anode + NaHCO₃ electrolyte + hydrophobic CO₂-permeable membrane
CO₂ from skin diffuses → changes pH → converts to PaCO₂ equivalent
Wide variation in slope + intercept between individuals — poor predictive value at extremes

PtcCO₂ − PaCO₂ Discrepancy — Martin et al.
Normocapnia: tcPCO₂ − PaCO₂ = 5 ± 4 mmHg. Hypercapnia: tcPCO₂ − PaCO₂ = 9 ± 6 mmHg. Progressive increase in discrepancy as PaCO₂ rises → CO₂ production exceeds removal. Factors: metabolic events in skin, circulatory disturbances, anaerobic heating coefficient of blood, ↑ CO₂ tension at ↑ electrode temperature. Clinical rule: compare with ABG especially during hypercapnia.
Skin burn risk: heated electrode → hyperpigmented macules; newer lower-temperature devices show modest discrepancies vs standard
Validated during HFOV: advantage over Petco₂ for HFOV patients — accurate during high-frequency oscillation
Useful during neonatal transport: continuous CO₂ trend without ABG
Best use: screening + trending ใน preterm with ↑ RR and small Vt where Petco₂ underestimates
4. Respiratory Monitoring
4.1 Flow Sensors
Pneumotachometer — Gold Standard for Flow/Volume
Fixed orifice type: pressure differential across fixed orifice ∝ square of flow rate
Laminar flow type: mesh screen/capillary network; pressure drop ∝ flow — linear relationship in calibrated range
Limitation in preterm: ETT leaks → all flow must pass through device; mask dead space + resistance → ↑ WOB; limited to pulmonary function testing or in-line with ETT
Hot-Wire Anemometer
Principle: heated element in airway circuit; flow cools element → electrical current maintains default temperature; current calibrated to flow
Advantages: high-frequency response, minimal resistive load, proven accuracy, bidirectional (second wire) — preferred for volume monitoring during mechanical ventilation including HFOV
Limitation: secretions on heated element → ↓ accuracy — clean or replace regularly
Thermistor/Thermocouple
Qualitative only: nonlinear, frequency-dependent relationship between peak amplitude and actual flow
Use: sleep lab only; used with chest wall motion sensors to identify presence/absence of flow (central vs obstructive apnea)
4.2 Impedance Monitoring

Impedance Apnea Monitor — Most Widely Used, Most Misunderstood
Principle: 2 electrodes on chest; high-frequency current >25 kHz through same ECG electrodes; air has higher impedance than tissue → inspiration = ↑ impedance (↑ gas + ↑ path length). CRITICAL limitation: CANNOT distinguish obstructive apnea from normal breathing — air shifts compartment-to-compartment during obstruction → impedance oscillates → NO ALARM despite airway obstruction + possible desaturation. Also: thoracic blood volume changes may mimic breathing. Sensitivity + predictive value: POOR for apnea in preterm (despite widespread use).
Emerging Impedance Technology
Machine learning — hidden Markov-based models: identify/predict apnea with bradycardia and/or hypoxemia → first step toward personalized risk assessment during hospitalization + after NICU discharge

Electrical Impedance Tomography (EIT): 8–32 electrodes circumferentially → sequential current → continuous high-resolution images of gas distribution throughout lung; identifies collapse, overdistension, ventilation distribution in mechanically ventilated patients; radiation-free
Future EIT role: bedside impedance monitoring → identify obstructive apnea in spontaneously breathing infants (not yet ready)
4.3 Respiratory Inductance Plethysmography (RIP)

RIP — Only Reliable Bedside Detection of Obstructive Apnea
Two bands: chest (rib cage = RC) + abdomen (AB) — sinusoidal inductance coils. Faraday's + Lenz's law: chest expansion → coil elongates → ↓ inductance → signal change. Key advantage: 2D respiratory model — obstructive apnea = asynchronous 180° out-of-phase RC vs AB movements (air moves one compartment → other while airway obstructed). Central apnea = flat baseline both channels. Semiquantitative volume waveform (Vt) with calibration algorithm — detects obstruction without oral/nasal flow sensor. Not currently at bedside but promising.
4.4 Diaphragm EMG (dEMG / Edi)
Transcutaneous dEMG: feasibility study in delivery room (moderately preterm) — useful indicator of RR, HR, diaphragm activity
Heart rate (dEMG): high-to-moderate agreement with ECG + pulse oximeter; respiratory rate: modest agreement with direct airflow measurement
Neural Edi (orogastric catheter): standard procedure during NIV-NAVA (neurally adjusted ventilatory assist) — provides continuous measure of neural respiratory drive + synchronizes ventilator
EMG amplitude may ↑ understanding of respiratory physiology + optimize respiratory support in future
5. Monitor Comparison — Quick Reference

rows: Skin burn; 15 min warm-up; ~15% missed events
Continuous O₂ trend; validate with ABG
Venous-weighted; poor inter-device reproducibility; no clear outcome benefit (BORN trial)
Cerebral O₂ research; NICU monitoring adjunct
Poor in pulmonary disease; not for HFOV; underestimates in preterm
ETT confirmation; trending CO₂ in stable infants
Overestimates in hypercapnia (+9 mmHg); skin burn
HFOV patients; transport; confirm with ABG
MISSES obstructive apnea — no alarm during obstruction
Not standard bedside; research/sleep lab only
Requires specialized software; research in most centers

blocks.text: 6. NICU Practical — ปรับ Monitor Settings ให้ถูกต้อง
6.1 Pulse Oximetry Setup — KCMH Protocol Consideration
Target SpO₂: 90–95% สำหรับ infant ที่ได้ supplemental O₂ (AAP) — verify NICU protocol ว่าตั้ง alarm ถูกหรือไม่
Averaging time: set 8 sec (อย่าใช้ 2 หรือ 16 sec routine — ทั้งคู่ suboptimal)
Alarm delay: 15 sec ใน stable preterm ที่ self-resolves; 0 sec ใน acute unstable infant
SpO₂ < 88%: reliability ต่ำ — ค่า displayed อาจ worse than reality หรือ better than reality. Always correlate with ABG ถ้า clinical concern
Post-transfusion: ↓ HbF → O₂ dissociation curve shifts right → same SpO₂ = better tissue delivery; อาจลด FiO₂ได้
6.2 Transcutaneous Monitoring
Rotate PtcO₂/PtcCO₂ probe q2h maximum ใน ELBW infant (ผิว fragile); q4h acceptable ใน larger preterm
Temperature 44°C; document site + time ทุก rotation
PtcCO₂ in HFOV: ใช้ PtcCO₂ แทน Petco₂ — more reliable during HFOV. Confirm with ABG q4–6h หรือเมื่อ setting เปลี่ยน
6.3 Apnea Monitoring
Impedance monitor alarm: DO NOT assume 'no alarm = no apnea' — obstructive apnea จะไม่ alarm แต่ SpO₂ อาจ desaturate
Always use SpO₂ + HR monitoring TOGETHER กับ impedance — SpO₂ เป็น de facto ตัวจับ obstructive apnea
Caffeine dose check: AoB (apnea of bradycardia) ที่ไม่ respond → verify dose, consider loading ใหม่, rule out infection/metabolic
6.4 BP Monitoring
MAP ≤30 mmHg: oscillometric BP accuracy unreliable — consider arterial line หรือ correlate with clinical signs (CRT, perfusion, UO, base deficit)
Cuff ขนาดถูกต้อง: width ~40% limb circumference; วัด upper arm right (pre-ductal) สำหรับ monitoring PDA/PPHN
UAC transducer: zero ทุกครั้งที่เปลี่ยน position infant; ระวัง transducer สูงกว่า level catheter → BP ต่ำ false
7. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Impedance Monitor ไม่ Detect Obstructive Apnea เลย
Impedance monitor เห็นอก-ท้อง 'ขยับ' ระหว่าง obstructive apnea (air ย้าย compartment) → ไม่ alarm. ทารก preterm อาจ desaturate เงียบๆ. SpO₂ + HR monitor คือ safety net ตัวจริง. ดังนั้น ใน infant ที่มี apnea of prematurity: อย่า rely on impedance monitor alone — ตั้ง SpO₂ alarm ที่ 87–89% + alarm delay ≤15 sec. RIP สามารถ detect obstructive ได้ดีกว่าแต่ไม่ available bedside.

PEARL 2 — Averaging Time 8 Sec: อย่าเปลี่ยนตามใจ
Averaging 2 sec → alarm ทุก movement. Averaging 16 sec → missed real hypoxemia events, underestimated nadir, merged events appear as 1 long episode → triggered FiO₂ ↑ ที่ไม่จำเป็น. 8 sec ดีที่สุดตาม evidence. Alarm delay 15 sec ↓ alarm burden 67% แต่ต้องมี nurse ratio เพียงพอ. ก่อน adjust settings ใดๆ ใน NICU → ต้องมี policy support.

PEARL 3 — SpO₂ <88%: ค่าที่อ่านได้อาจ Worse หรือ Better กว่าความเป็นจริง
In 1664 preterm infants: <40% ของ readings อยู่ภายใน 3% ของ true SaO₂ เมื่อ SpO₂ <88%. ดังนั้น SpO₂ 85% จาก monitor อาจหมายถึง SaO₂ 82% หรือ 88% ก็ได้. เมื่อ infant ดู worse than SpO₂ suggests → trust clinical picture และทำ ABG. อย่า normalize SpO₂ 85% โดยไม่ correlate clinical context.

PEARL 4 — PtcCO₂ ดีกว่า Petco₂ ใน HFOV
HFOV: tidal volume เล็กมาก + RR สูงมาก → Petco₂ underestimates PaCO₂ หรืออ่านไม่ได้เลย. PtcCO₂ validated ใน HFOV — accurate trend. แต่จำ: tcPCO₂ − PaCO₂ = 5 mmHg ใน normocapnia และ 9 mmHg ใน hypercapnia → ต้อง calibrate กับ ABG ทุก 4–6h. ใน transport + HFOV: PtcCO₂ = monitor of choice.

PEARL 5 — NIRS = Tissue O₂, NOT Arterial O₂
rSO₂ = HbO₂/(HbO₂+HHb) — เป็น venous-weighted tissue saturation, normal range 55–85% ใน extremely preterm (ไม่ใช่ 90–95%). SpO₂ ปกติแต่ rSO₂ ต่ำ = tissue extraction ↑ (hypoperfusion, ↓CO, cardiac compromise). BORN trial: NIRS-guided therapy ไม่ลด death/brain injury vs usual care — ยังไม่ใช่ standard of care; ใช้เป็น adjunct monitoring เท่านั้น.
8. Evidence Update — Key Studies

Di Fiore JM et al J Perinatol 2012;32:280: comparison of SpO₂ averaging times in preterm; 8-sec averaging optimal — reduces short-event alarms while minimizing falsely prolonged events; 15-sec delay ↓ alarms by 67%
Poets CF et al Pediatrics 1993;91:784: accuracy of pulse oximetry in preterm infants; 1664 infants; mean SaO₂ − SpO₂ = −1.84 ± 2.93%; <40% within 3% when SpO₂ < 88% — established accuracy limits
Dawson JA et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2014;99:F393: skin melanin: no significant effect on SpO₂ accuracy in neonates; modest overestimation possible in Black infants (unlike adults)

Sink DW et al Pediatrics 2022;149:e2021054046: home baby monitor pulse oximetry: sensitivity 0–88.8% for hypoxemia vs Masimo Radical 7; bradycardia sensitivity 0% for one marketed monitor — not FDA regulated; caution essential
Poets CF et al Pediatrics 1994;94:878: PtcO₂ vs SpO₂: ~15% hypoxemic and hyperoxemic events missed by PtcO₂; sensitivity for hypoxemia (<50 mmHg) 68–100%; 16–30 sec response delay vs 3–4 sec for SpO₂
Martin RJ et al J Perinatol 2002;22:468: tcPCO₂ − PaCO₂ = 5 ± 4 mmHg normocapnia; 9 ± 6 mmHg hypercapnia — progressive discrepancy at higher PaCO₂ levels
Rüdiger M et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2005;90:F208: transcutaneous CO₂ accurate during HFOV in neonates — recommended as monitoring modality when capnography unreliable
Hyttel-Sørensen S et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2015;100:F51: NIRS: normative range rSO₂ = 55–85% in extremely preterm; term = 77.9 ± 8.5%; influenced by GA, position, age

BORN Trial (Hyttel-Sørensen S et al) Lancet 2023;402:905: n=1601 extremely preterm; cerebral oximetry-guided care (first 72h) vs usual care: NO difference in death or severe brain injury at 36 wk PMA — NIRS monitoring alone insufficient to change outcomes
ISO 80601-2-85:2021: International Organization for Standardization — standard for NIRS medical electrical equipment to improve inter-manufacturer comparability
Southall DP et al Pediatrics 1982;71:867: impedance apnea monitoring in preterm infants: POOR sensitivity and predictive value for apnea — established foundational limitation of impedance technology
Poets CF et al Arch Dis Child 1993;68:467: impedance monitoring misses obstructive apnea — no alarm during complete airway obstruction while abdominal + chest movement continue

Oliveira A et al Physiol Meas 2016;37:1204: hidden Markov-based models for impedance data — identify/predict apnea + bradycardia + hypoxemia clusters; first step toward personalized NICU risk assessment

Frerichs I et al Thorax 2017;72:83: EIT consensus statement: 8–32 electrodes; continuous images of gas distribution; identifies collapse, overdistension, ventilation distribution; radiation-free; clinical evidence growing in mechanically ventilated neonates

Shalish W et al J Pediatr 2016;179:110: Edi catheter (NIV-NAVA) provides neural respiratory drive + patient-ventilator synchrony — part of standard NIV-NAVA care; ↑ understanding of respiratory physiology

Moorman JR et al Lancet 2011;378:1928: HeRO (heart rate characteristics) monitoring in VLBW infants: reduced mortality by alerting to early deterioration days before clinical signs; ↓ 22% in-hospital mortality (RCT, n=3003)
AAP Committee on Fetus and Newborn Pediatrics 2016;138:e20161802: SpO₂ target 90–95% for preterm receiving supplemental O₂ — standard for alarm setting in NICU
9. Self-Check Q&A
Q: ทำไม impedance apnea monitor ถึงไม่ alarm ระหว่าง obstructive apnea?

ตอบ: Obstructive apnea: airway ปิดแต่ diaphragm ยังทำงาน → air ย้ายระหว่าง chest ↔ abdomen → thoracic impedance ยังเปลี่ยนแปลง (oscillates) → monitor interpret ว่า 'breathing' → ไม่ alarm. ทำให้ obstructive apnea + SpO₂ desaturation เกิดโดยไม่มี notification. แก้ไข: ต้องใช้ SpO₂ monitoring ร่วมเสมอ; RIP สามารถ detect ได้จาก asynchronous RC vs AB.
Q: Averaging time 8 sec vs 2 sec vs 16 sec: ผลต่อ event detection คืออะไร?

ตอบ: 2 sec: alarm บ่อย จาก motion + short self-resolving events → alert fatigue. 8 sec: optimal — ลด short-event nuisance alarms โดย preserve real events. 16 sec: distorts waveform — eliminates true short events, reduces nadir severity, merges events into one long episode → FiO₂ ↑ ที่ไม่จำเป็น. 15-sec alarm delay ↓ alarms 67% แต่ต้องมี adequate nurse staffing.
Q: PtcO₂ ต่างจาก SpO₂ อย่างไร และในสถานการณ์ใดที่ PtcO₂ เหนือกว่า?

ตอบ: PtcO₂ measures PaO₂ (dissolved O₂ in plasma); SpO₂ measures % Hb occupied by O₂ (arterial). PtcO₂ advantages: stable during motion; continuous PaO₂ trend (not just saturation). SpO₂ advantages: immediate response (3–4 sec vs 16–30 sec), no skin burn risk, no warm-up time. PtcO₂ useful: ต้องการ trend PaO₂ continuously (hyperoxia risk monitoring ใน ELBW); motion artifact interferes with SpO₂. Always ใช้ร่วมกับ ABG เพราะ ~15% of events missed.
Q: NIRS rSO₂ = 65% ใน preterm 28 wk: interpret อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Normative range: 55–85% ใน extremely preterm → 65% อยู่ใน normal range. Remember: rSO₂ ≠ SpO₂ — เป็น venous-weighted tissue saturation ปกติจะต่ำกว่า SpO₂ มาก. Interpret in context: ถ้า SpO₂ 92% + rSO₂ 65% = delivery-extraction balance OK. ถ้า SpO₂ 95% + rSO₂ 45% = tissue oxygen extraction ↑ → suspect ↓ cerebral perfusion (hypotension, PDA, IVH). BORN trial: NIRS-guided therapy ไม่ ↓ death/brain injury → ยังใช้เป็น adjunct เท่านั้น.
Q: Colorimetric CO₂ detector ใช้ยืนยัน ETT placement ได้อย่างไรและอะไรที่ detect ไม่ได้?

ตอบ: Metacresol purple → yellow เมื่อ CO₂ ≥0.54%; placed between ventilator + ETT. ยืนยัน tracheal placement ได้ CANNOT detect: (1) hypocarbia, (2) hypercarbia, (3) right mainstem bronchus intubation — ยัง yellow เพราะมี CO₂ แต่ ventilation asymmetric, (4) oropharyngeal placement ใน spontaneously breathing patient. ดังนั้น ใช้ร่วมกับ chest rise, auscultation, CXR เพื่อ confirm ETT position.
Q: Oscillometric BP overestimates หรือ underestimates ใน preterm?

ตอบ: Generally overestimates vs invasive arterial line, เฉพาะ ใน smaller infants at lower BP values. MAP ≤30 mmHg: accuracy greatly diminished → noninvasive ไม่น่าเชื่อถือในสถานการณ์ที่สำคัญ (hypotension, shock). Clinical pearl: infant ที่ดู shock แต่ NIBP ดูปกติ → ทำ ABG + assess CRT + UO + base deficit + consider arterial line; cuff width ต้องถูก (40% limb circumference) ไม่งั้นค่า false high.
Q: HeRO system คืออะไรและมีหลักฐานอะไรสนับสนุน?

ตอบ: HeRO = Heart Rate Observation: longitudinal regression model ของ HRV patterns ที่ associate กับ systemic infection + inflammation — แสดง early warning ก่อน clinical deterioration. RCT (Moorman Lancet 2011, n=3003 VLBW): ↓ in-hospital mortality 22% with HeRO display vs controls. Mechanism: infection → cytokine → autonomic nervous system → ↓ HRV (reduced heart rate variation + transient decelerations pattern). ไม่ใช่ standard ทุก NICU แต่ validated benefit ใน VLBW.
Q: EIT คืออะไรและมี potential อะไรใน NICU?

ตอบ: Electrical Impedance Tomography: 8–32 electrodes circumferentially รอบทรวงอก → small alternating currents sequentially → reconstruct continuous high-resolution images of gas distribution in lung. Identifies: lung collapse, overdistension, ventilation distribution asymmetry — radiation-free. ปัจจุบัน: ใช้ใน mechanically ventilated patients. Future: bedside obstructive apnea detection ใน spontaneous breathing infants — promising แต่ not yet ready for routine NICU use.

Day 135 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.38
บทต่อไป: Chapter 38 — Diagnostic Imaging of the Neonate. เตรียมตัว: plain radiograph interpretation (CXR, AXR) ใน NICU; chest X-ray patterns: RDS vs pneumonia vs MAS vs PTX vs BPD vs CLD; abdominal X-ray: NEC patterns (pneumatosis, portal gas, free air); cranial ultrasound: IVH grading (Papile), PVL, white matter injury, cerebellar hemorrhage; echocardiography for congenital heart disease + PDA assessment; radiation safety principles ใน neonates (ALARA).
